verses.commentary: Worthy living matches the divine calling received. Paul's imprisonment underscores the calling's costliness. Vocation demands corresponding lifestyle; belief must produce behavior.
Humility, gentleness, patience, and bearing with one another in love are relational virtues. These qualities enable community life. Christian character is others-oriented, not self-focused.
Eager effort maintains Spirit-given unity through peace's bond. Unity is preserved, not created; the Spirit makes it, believers keep it. Peace binds the community together.
One body and one Spirit correspond to one hope. Unity rests on theological reality: one people, one divine presence, one destiny. Diversity exists within fundamental oneness.
One Lord, one faith, one baptism emphasize singular allegiance, belief system, and initiation rite. Christian identity is unified around Christ, truth, and sacrament.
One God and Father over all, through all, in all, encapsulates divine transcendence, immanence, and omnipresence. God's universal sovereignty unifies all existence.
Grace is individually apportioned according to Christ's measure. Unity does not mean uniformity; each receives distinct grace. Christ distributes gifts personally and purposefully.
Christ's ascension distributed gifts to people. Ascending high, He led captives and gave gifts. The victorious Christ enriches His church with spiritual endowments.
Ascension implies prior descent to earth's lower regions. Christ's incarnation preceded exaltation. Humiliation and glory are sequential stages in Christ's mission.
The One who descended is the same who ascended above all heavens to fill all things. Christ's cosmic supremacy follows earthly humiliation. Universal filling requires complete trajectory.
Christ gave apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, and teachers. These offices are Christ's gifts to the church. Leadership is grace, not achievement or election.
Leaders equip God's people for service to build up Christ's body. Leadership's purpose is empowerment, not control. The body is built as all members serve.
Unity in faith and knowledge of God's Son leads to maturity, measured by Christ's fullness. Growth is communal and Christward. Maturity means becoming like Christ.
Maturity replaces infancy, preventing doctrinal instability. Children are easily deceived by false teaching. Stability in truth protects from cunning deception.
Speaking truth in love produces growth toward Christ, the head. Truth without love is harsh; love without truth is weak. Both are necessary for healthy development.
The whole body grows from Christ through every joint and ligament as each part works. Growth is organic, coordinated, and universal. Every member contributes to corporate maturity.
Paul insists believers no longer live like Gentiles in futile thinking. Conversion demands lifestyle change. Mindset transformation produces behavioral transformation.
Darkened understanding and hardened hearts separate from God's life. Ignorance is culpable, not innocent. Spiritual blindness is moral, not merely intellectual.
Lost sensitivity leads to sensuality and greed-driven impurity. Moral numbness produces unchecked sin. Conscience erosion enables escalating evil.
Learning Christ contradicts the pagan lifestyle. Christ-education is moral transformation, not information. Knowing Christ changes behavior.
Hearing and being taught in Jesus means instruction according to truth. Christian teaching centers on Jesus. Truth in Jesus is the standard for life.
The old self, corrupted by deceitful desires, must be put off. Conversion involves divestment of former identity. The old self is being destroyed by its own lusts.
Renewal in the mind's attitude is ongoing transformation. Mental renewal is essential to life change. The spirit of the mind directs thought patterns.
The new self, created like God in righteousness and holiness, must be put on. New identity reflects God's character. True righteousness and holiness are divine likeness.
Putting off falsehood and speaking truth to neighbors flows from being members of one another. Honesty is communal necessity. Deception harms the body.
Anger without sin is possible if not prolonged beyond sunset. Righteous anger can exist, but nurturing it invites sin. Quick resolution prevents anger's corruption.
Unresolved anger gives the devil a foothold. Satan exploits bitterness. Guarding the heart against prolonged anger protects from demonic influence.
Thieves must stop stealing and work to share with the needy. Conversion reverses behavior: from taking to giving. Honest labor enables generosity.
Unwholesome talk must be replaced with words that build up and benefit listeners. Speech should construct, not destroy. Words minister grace to hearers.
Grieving the Holy Spirit is possible through sinful behavior. The Spirit sealed believers for redemption; sin offends Him. Living worthy honors the Spirit's presence.
Bitterness, rage, anger, brawling, slander, and malice must be removed. These destroy community. Negative emotions and speech have no place in believers.
Kindness, compassion, and forgiveness should characterize believers, mirroring God's forgiveness in Christ. Christian ethics imitate divine behavior. Forgiveness received enables forgiveness given.
